Hey everybody, my name is Brent Co-Peters. Welcome to the training today. I have a special guest with me. I've got Scott from the James Whalen Agency and Scott's there in southern Florida, aren't you Scott? Yeah, Brent, how you doing? Good, good. Hey, yeah, thank you for being here. Thanks. I didn't really give you a introduction. I should have got a little more information about that. I was able to hang out with Scott about two weeks ago in San Francisco and just had a really good visit with him. We've been doing business with Scott for about two years already and I look forward to doing a lot more business with you. Scott's fairly recently newly wed and has some plans to have a nice trip this summer, a little honeymoon this summer. To give just an awesome feedback, Scott's been a trusted partner of ours that we've worked with and have had tremendous success. As we were putting together this training program, we were talking about what's the best information, what's the best kind of training we can provide. Russell actually suggested, hey, I want to get an interview with Scott. Scott, thank you so much for taking the time out of your busy day to meet with me and share some of the information that you're doing that are helping people like me and the folks by watching this who need additional help in solo advertising. It's been definitely effective for our business and we want to help share some more information with our guests. Yeah, absolutely. As far as the agency here, that's our primary focus is the direct response advertisers and the solo slash dedicated email has become such a valuable resource and medium for our clients because especially with the dedicated emails, you're reaching the most loyal individuals within a certain media property or outlet. They're the ones that have stood up, raised their hand and said, yes, I want to receive your information in my inbox and by doing so, they agreed to receive our third party emails and many, many clients have taken great advantage of that for sure. That's awesome. Scott, I'm just curious, how long have you been kind of in this advertising space and doing stuff like this? Well, myself, I've been in advertising for about 10 years, a little over 10 years back in 2003's where I started, kind of more of a radio background back then and four years ago I came over to the James R. Wayland agency and was really introduced to more of the digital online space as far as the direct response driven campaigns and really from there, we've really taken that to a whole new level with our clients. With direct response advertising, we're held to such accountability for the campaigns that we run that being that we are able to produce results, it's easy for our relationship with our clients to grow gradually or quite quickly depending on the offer that we're running. That's the power of the direct response, advertising is, I love it and I told you about it. We can actually run an email, a dedicated email and within a few hours for some of these mailings, we can know if we're positive on that mailing so far and to see that response is fantastic. For sure, with Solo Email Sends, it is more of a risk reward type of relationship with the publisher where a digital online banner campaign will take you about 30 days, if not more to receive the data you're looking for let alone the revenue from the campaign where like you said with a dedicated email, sometimes within two hours you know if you've had a successful campaign, worst case maybe 48 to 72 hours you'll get the full results from the campaign and be able to recoup your investment and then turn the money over to the next campaign. Yeah, that's fantastic. From your experience and how long you've been obviously with the James William agency and looking at dedicated and Solo Email, how has advertising changed? How has dedicated email space maybe changed a little bit in the last four or five years? It's changed in the sense that I would say maybe even four or five years ago or even more, clients could be more expected to receive a much larger upfront ROI, the three to one, four to ones where I think where the space was new and people, clients, advertisers really weren't hitting the lists and with much less frequency where today while it's still a valuable tool, many of our clients have the understanding that while they still can get those large upfront returns that it's more about their funnel and their back end and more acquiring the acquisition so that they can monetize it hopefully over a short amount of time but nonetheless post campaign. Okay, yeah, that's fun. We talked about the funnel system and it had you in the future of your front end offers and off sales and even down sales and back end offer. The offer that we've done really well with you is our supplement offer. We had a tweak that. We had a trial we started with initially and we'd sell into continuity and when we first initially tried nailing with that, that's the offer. It just took a long time to recoup those costs for our advertising and when we switched that model up a little bit and we're selling multiple bottles of our supplement upfront, we can see that return on that and then very quickly pay for that ad and then through our upsells and then there's something we sold our customers during additional revenue which made it even more successful for us. Yeah, and that all comes like you said from your tests, starting back at your launch phase before you even buy any paid media and then constantly be testing which you guys have done very well where you can utilize A, B splits, being able to test different copies to one email send if the publisher allows it. Not all of them do but the ones that do, I always recommend that especially because if you're in the testing phase you're able to use a control that you know will probably make up for any inadequacies of the test and if the test does well then the overall campaign as a whole was going to do even that much better but that also allows you to scale up much quicker once you've honed it in and you have a much more comfort level because ultimately your return, your ROI is based on the price you're paying for the campaign and if you're able to have the comfortability in a certain media outlet you can then do larger ad buys for better rates and then effectively be improving your ROI while still doing the same thing. Yeah, the split test, we talked about in the kind of the training that I offer here kind of briefly and over that but you know having that initial control, I think the initial split test we ran with you guys, we were testing subject lines and so we had same copies, same email copy and links there or different links for tracking purposes but we had different subjects and so we tested them, we kind of felt like we found the correct subject and then we found the winner with that subject and then we started changing this in the body copy, we had a longer version and a shorter version and we found this offer at least with the list that we were testing, the shorter copy was doing better and so we kind of tweaked that and so now you know now we feel like we have a greater control there, now we want to increase that and opt out again so we'll continue testing again additional. Yeah and even bringing up subject lines, you know you can have the best offer, the best back end, the best funnel but if you're not getting people to open up the emails, you know you're handicapping yourself to a point where you'll never receive your ROI, the return you need upfront or later on. So even split testing the subject lines is a great idea. I kind of see it as like a four, at least a four prong effect where you start with the subject line because if you don't get people to open up the emails they're never going to then click on your copy or they will so you find you'll be getting great conversions but you just need more people so that's actually a good problem if that's even a thing, a good problem but that's something that we can work with as you know with you because if the conversions are good but we're not getting enough people to open we can work on the subject line and get more people to the offer and then of course once you get the audience clicking on your offer you then are relied upon your offer, the actual offer and how strong the offer is because really what the media company can do you know we'll get as many people as we can to your offer, to your landing page from there you know hopefully it is a good fit for the audience but you have to rely on your offer and then they go into your funnel so I see those as the four of course most important steps starting with the subject line you know and of course picking media outlets that are in line with the individuals that would be purchasing your product. How many companies, how many publishers are publishing companies do you guys work with just from the J&J Wheeling Agency? Well at the agency we have probably 20 to 30 different exclusive publishers where we're the actual sales force for but we are also a full-scale ad agency and do media buying all across the board for our clients even outside of the dedicated email and going to TV, radio, print and everything else so I say there's not a publisher that we don't work with but we have different levels as far as exclusivity, close partnerships and then just straight up media buying. That's great you know and you just recently just tested it you know kind of a bigger I guess a publisher that you know that you guys didn't work with exclusively but you guys were able to reach out there and get us a mailing and actually we were able to get that really quickly just happened to work out that way. Yeah and I think also I just you had actually sent me another property and I just got you some dates for it so I'll probably send it over to you a little bit but yeah that's what we can do and what I tell when I told you like I tell most of our clients when we're going outside of our exclusives they're just doing media buying I'd say 99% of the time will probably get you a better rate if not the same rate as if you were to call yourself so our goal is just to take a lot of the workload off of your day to day so that you can work on what's most important for your company and that's once we get you the individuals to your offer that you're converting them and keeping them over time so it takes it kind of separates the workload for you. So can you kind of share it you know just briefly how does I mean how does the payment work how does in terms of you know you guys's commission I mean is that obviously I mean whatever you can share just a little bit just so people haven't understanding what they're paying for and maybe how that breaks up just like just basically. Yeah again with our exclusive properties we are the publisher so there's no type of commission type of process if we're going outside of the exclusives the the media property we're acting on behalf as an agency for you so there are agency commissions built in on the publisher side so there's really no extra costs to a client of ours that utilizes us to do media buying. If there are additional costs it could be for creatives and stuff like that that don't necessarily have to do with the actual media placement. not that the loss as far as revenue is great but if you can use that to to turn around so much more quickly and I'll use a banner campaign as as a for instance where if we're running an ad on say wnd.com at 300 by 250 and it's a new client I want to run as many different creatives as possible eight to ten different ones if if if they have it because even if after the week or the month the they spent $5,000 on the campaign it came back $3,000 total revenue and they had $2,000 loss if you can look into those creatives and and even if it's just three of the creatives within those 10 were positive if you were to scale just those three up you'd have a positive overall campaign so it's again it's learning about what what you've just done and not being so quick to to abandon it um like I said that that that's just one one uh it kind of important information there there's so many so many other things just even going to like I said the subject line and um something folks don't realize trying new concepts within the vsl not just a straight up you know sales so they're they're always trying to to mold something new actually I just had a client that just did that and they were kind of bummed because the the new concept that they just that they just rolled out only matched their control and I said that's that's awesome and they're like what are you talking about because in the way I explained it is it took you eight years to get your control and now in 30 days you've come to the concept that matched your control so imagine what you can do in the next year and and and that's the whole concept is that learning from the data you receive really is so important that's great you know I think looking back to I think recognizing that you know with anything you know it with and that's not to say that we can't run campaigns that are female targeted or or not political in nature at all because we do plenty of that just most of our exclusive properties tend to be in that niche such as world meddaily.com sites like Michael Savage independent living news GOP USA conservative contacts tea party.org you can kind of see the theme of where those those lists are going and and and some of the lists are 75,000 subs upwards of over a million subscribers and what we've done because you're going to have different prices based on how how big the lists are or how little they are but we've tried to price everything so that your expected response is is equal so just because a list has a million names doesn't mean that it's going to be more expensive than a list that has half a million names if the list with half a million performs better but either way we again we price it to optimize your response. That's great you know I think too you know I'm going back to you to really talk about earlier you know it doesn't help you guys and the answer you know you want to build a long-term relationship with with people like ourselves you know and it doesn't need very good to have companies one-offs you know you want to be able to have help people achieve success to build a book and back and they're buying to buy advertising with you guys that's that's the benefit for everybody isn't it? Well yeah exactly our our company can't grow with one-offs and so our success is our our client successes we could you know we can only grow with with their growth so to have one-offs although it does happen sometimes the offer is not ready to be put to mass market or tweaks need to be made or whatever the case may be but ultimately it does us as an agency no no good to to have a short-minded focus it's more the long-term and not just getting the first deal but getting the next like with us so far next two years worth of deals and even with us it was you know we it was a slower process we did a lot of testing not everybody can scale up to a million-dollar budgets within the first six months or the year some can it's just all different and kind of just growing the relationship as the foundation opposed to to the revenue because then then we can really work together to achieve both our goals that's awesome so if you're maybe you know it's been tips some of starting out right now don't like giving recommendations uneducated on what they're you know what who they're trying to reach you know I can have the best list in the world but it's not the best list for the offer so you know that that's the first step and for us you know our sales cycle isn't like the oil and gas industry where it's I call you and I got you know I call you today and you got the best offer and you got to act today you know we take a we take a much I don't want to say slower response or a slower rate of response but we like to make sure that that is the right fit and you know our typical sales cycle can be 15 to 30 days from first contact so so it's not a quick turnover we're not looking to just get the money and get you out there you know we want to find the right fit if it happens sooner that's fine but especially with dedicated emails solo emails you're probably going to be about two to three weeks out for booking purposes anyways just scheduling because the lists don't send you know 14 emails a day you know at most maybe two and even then that's a lot so there is time involved and again but once the campaign goes out you can you can recoup and get your results fairly quickly awesome
